tier1_description: High-stakes files requiring full TDD ceremony before production edits. (Service preset; copy from .tdd/presets/library.json or .tdd/presets/cli.json for other project types.)
_test_file_policy_doc: Phase-aware test-file policy. Replaces the older allow_test_file_edits_without_gate flag. Test edits are allowed during spec/red phases (writing failing tests), denied after red is confirmed (the documented 'don't edit tests in green phase' rule). The legacy allow_after_red_confirmed boolean is DEPRECATED in v1.7.x (use post_red_mechanical_update typed exceptions instead) and will be removed in v2.0.0. The hook emits a stderr deprecation warning each invocation when the boolean is consulted.

post_red_mechanical_update._doc: v1.7.0 typed-exception system, v1.8.0 AST-validated. Replaces the all-or-nothing boolean above with operator-authorized, mechanically-validated, auditable per-cycle exceptions for post-red test-edit work (e.g., signature widening that breaks 12 pre-existing call sites). Default enabled:false — opt-in for safe rollout. v1.8.0 adds: max_per_cycle cap, schema_predicate_correction type (operators add to exception_types), AST helper at scripts/tdd/ast/validator.go (Go ≥1.26.2; falls back to regex when unavailable).

_markers_doc: Four-marker TDD ceremony. Edit-time hooks (require-tdd-state, require-second-opinion Tier-1 leg) require the first three; commit-time hook (gate-tier1-commit) additionally requires the fourth. Setting any marker without the matching operator APPROVED reply is self-approval and is forbidden.
_marker_aliases_doc: Backwards-compat aliases. The hook accepts the old name but emits a stderr deprecation warning. Drop in next major version (2026-Q3 target).
_legacy_required_markers_doc: Used by the existing require-tdd-state.sh path for backwards-compat. Reads the new edit-time markers; old name is honored via alias.
_integration_guards_doc: Project-level static-analysis guards. Each guard is a regex pattern that, if matched in a file outside allowed_globs, fails the green commit. Use to encode invariants that aren't easily expressed as integration tests (e.g., 'no direct calls to API X outside wrapper Y'). Guards are FALLBACK protection — write integration tests first; add a guard for invariants tests can't reach. See .claude/rules/go-integration-guards.md for the decision tree.

_integration_guards_schema.name: short identifier shown in violation message
_integration_guards_schema.pattern: POSIX extended regex (grep -E), matched against file content
_integration_guards_schema.severity: deny | warn — deny blocks the commit; warn only logs
_integration_guards_schema.allowed_globs: list of glob patterns; files matching any are exempt. Supports ** for cross-directory.
_integration_guards_schema.rationale: one sentence explaining what invariant this guards

_integration_guards_exclude_dirs_doc: Directories the integration-guards scanner will skip. Default (when this field is absent) for downstream consumers: ['.git', 'vendor', 'node_modules', '.tdd', '.claude'] — those dirs hold the installed starter pack in downstream projects, and you don't want guards designed for YOUR project firing on the starter's code. The pack itself sets this to '[.git, vendor, node_modules, .tdd]' — keeps .tdd excluded (self-referential match: this config file IS in .tdd and contains the guard patterns themselves) but allows scanning .claude/ where the pack's own production hook/skill code lives.
_integration_guards_examples_doc: Each project should add its own guards over time — every shipped bug that a grep could have caught becomes a permanent guard. The defaults below are PACK-SELF guards (encoding invariants for the starter pack itself, since this repo IS the pack); downstream Go projects should REPLACE these with their own project-specific invariants.

integration_guards.name: no_hardcoded_user_home_path
integration_guards.rationale: Hardcoded user paths break on other developers' machines. Use $PROJECT_ROOT, $PWD, $HOME, or relative paths. Reported via parasitoid trial pre-scan; surfaced two real test bugs on first scan.

integration_guards.name: no_eval_invocation_in_hooks
integration_guards.rationale: eval in hook scripts is a code-injection risk — input may flow from tool args under attacker control.

integration_guards.name: no_silent_error_suppression_in_hooks
integration_guards.rationale: Hook scripts must fail-fast (set -euo pipefail). 'set +e' silently ignores failures and lets the hook return success despite a real problem.

integration_guards.name: no_chmod_777_in_pack
integration_guards.rationale: World-writable permissions are a security risk. The starter pack denies chmod 777 in user code (guard-dangerous-bash.sh) and must not use it itself.

integration_guards.name: no_curl_pipe_shell_in_pack
integration_guards.rationale: curl|bash pipes remote code into a shell with no integrity check. The pack denies this in user code and must not use it itself.

_second_opinion_doc: Default Codex model for the /second-opinion skill. Resolution order: env var (SECOND_OPINION_MODEL_TIER1 / SECOND_OPINION_MODEL_DEFAULT / SECOND_OPINION_FALLBACK_MODEL) > this config block > skill's hardcoded fallback. Defaults below pin the most powerful model for both tiers — change to the latest available when a newer Codex model ships. Note: model_tier1 (gpt-5.5) requires ChatGPT-account auth via 'codex login'; with API-key auth the skill auto-falls-back to fallback_model.
_second_opinion_v1_6_doc: v1.6.0 Tier-1 artifact requirements. All three default to false for safe opt-in rollout; flip to true after the eval harness shows value for your codebase (see docs/specs/second-opinion-v1.6.0-spec.md §8 validation plan). The skill writes these artifacts when their requirement flag is true OR when Pass A is enabled (env var SECOND_OPINION_PASS_A_DISABLE=1 disables Pass A independently of the require flag).

second_opinion._size_threshold_doc: Layer-0 commit-time check in gate-tier1-commit.sh: any commit with churn (added + removed lines) above size_threshold_lines requires fresh /second-opinion adjudication, regardless of Tier 1/cycle state. Catches large refactors on non-Tier-1 paths that the Tier-1-specific gate misses. Set to 0 (or negative) to disable. Default 50 — calibrated for routine work; bump higher for projects that commit large vendored content or big generated files.
second_opinion._require_hash_binding_tier1_doc: F5: bind /second-opinion adjudication to specific diff/plan content. When true AND path is Tier 1, require-second-opinion.sh denies if recorded diff_sha256 (sha of `git diff HEAD --cached`) or plan_sha256 (sha of .tdd/current-plan.md) doesn't match current. Closes the bypass where a fresh adjudication for one diff silently covers later unrelated work. Killswitch env var: SECOND_OPINION_HASH_DISABLE=1.
second_opinion._no_discretion_doc: v1.9.0+ no-discretion mode. When enabled:true, the v1.9 trigger hooks (plan/test/production triggers + bash pretrigger + posttool backstop + session-stop-review) own the review gate. Since v1.9.4, the legacy require-second-opinion.sh defers entirely in this mode. scripts/tdd/run-second-opinion.sh is the only legitimate Codex caller. required_for sub-flags gate which write classes create a pending obligation; max_review_rounds_per_cycle (since v1.9.1, default 4) caps iterations to prevent fix-introduces-regression spirals. ENABLED ON THE STARTER PACK ITSELF AS OF v1.9.5 — the pack now self-enforces.

_enforcement_mode_doc: F6: graduated enforcement for the 4 process-discipline gates (gate-tier1-commit, require-second-opinion, require-tdd-state, guard-bash-pipefail). Values: 'strict' (default; deny + exit 2), 'warn' (stderr advisory + allow exit 0), 'off' (silent passthrough). Per-hook override in enforcement_mode_overrides wins over global. Invalid values fall back to 'strict' with a stderr warning (typo can't soften). Security gates (guard-dangerous-bash, guard-protected-files, scan-for-secrets) ignore this — strict-only by design.
_trivial_paths_doc: F13: canonical 'skip second opinion' glob list. Consumed by require-second-opinion.sh's is_always_allowed_path() AND the /second-opinion skill's Step 3 filter loop. Each consumer falls back to its own inline list if this field is missing/empty OR jq is unavailable (no regression for environments without jq). require-tdd-state.sh INTENTIONALLY does NOT consult this list (per cycle f4 — it must govern pack-internal markdown like .claude/skills/second-opinion/SKILL.md, which would be skipped if *.md was here). Edge case: setting this to [] is treated as 'use fallback'; to truly disable, set to a single placeholder pattern that matches nothing.
